overview_table.Features: Unified Model Magical Appraisal Ability Improvement
Improvise II Spell Widgets I Ability Improvement

features.name: Arcane Battlecasting
features.description:
As an Artificer, you are able to prepare and cast spells from the Arcane
spell list:

- **Spells Prepared.** At 1st Rank, you may prepare 4 spells for this
  Class at the end of each long rest. You may cast those prepared spells
  using this feature.

- **Class Circle Cap.** Each spell you prepare may have a maximum
  baseline Circle of 1st Circle.

- **Heightening.** When you cast spells using this feature, you may
  heighten spells up to your Class Circle Cap.

- **Spell Points.** At 1st Rank, you gain 2 Spell Points. When you cast
  spells using this feature, you must expend Spell Points equal to the
  Circle of the spell. Spell Points are regained at the end of each long
  rest.

- **Spellcasting Modifier.** Your spellcasting modifier when casting
  spells using this feature is INT.

  - **Your Spell Attack Bonus** = your INT + your Tier Bonus

  - **Your Spell Save DC** = 8 + your INT + your Tier Bonus

  - **Spellcasting Checks** made with spells prepared for this class use
    your INT.

- **Signature Spell.** Your designated signature spell may be one of the
  1st Circle or higher spells prepared for this feature.

- **Higher Ranks.** At higher Ranks, the spells prepared, Spell Points,
  and Class Circle Cap all improve as described in the Class Overview
  Table.

features.name: Artificer Field
features.flavor_text: What you would consider unstable experimentation is what I call unlocking its true potential.
features.description:
At 1st Rank, you choose an Artificer Field to study as you invent new
magical creations. You gain features from this chosen Artificer Field at
1st, 4th, and 8th Rank. Your Artificer Field will *specialize* you for
creating certain types of items:

- **Consumable.** Artificer Fields *specialized in consumables* grant
  new stock recipes and modify existing stock recipes.

- **Equipment.** Artificer Fields *specialized in equipment* create,
  upgrade, and modify armaments, wearables, or other equipment.

## Overload Die

Some Artificer features use your *overload die*, which starts at 1d10.
At higher Ranks, your overload die improves as described in the Class
Overview Table.

features.name: Innovation
features.flavor_text: You begin tinkering with arcane alchemy, complex machinery, and quantised magic.
features.description:
At 1st Rank, you have a number of *innovation points (IP)* equal to your
Artificer Rank. At the end of each long rest, you may freely *assign*
your IP using your Artificer features, including those granted by your
Artificer Field. Benefits gained from IP persist while the IP is
assigned to them. As a free action, you may also *unassign* some or all
of your IP, which then become available to assign again at the end of
your next long rest.

features.name: Improvise I
features.flavor_text: Your greatest strength is your mind, and your weapon is your slapdash collection of Arcane doodads.
features.description:
At 2nd Rank, at the end of each rest, you may create a Common gadget of
your choosing that is *improvised*. An improvised gadget is destroyed at
the end of your next rest.

features.name: Resourceful Assembly
features.description:
At 2nd Rank, you gain the following benefits:

- **Ingenuity.** When you roll your resource die for a value check to
  start a crafting project, you may replace the outcome with your
  Artificer Rank.

- **Overtime.** When you take the Craft downtime activity, you may make
  an additional Craft check to add progress to another one of your
  crafting projects. You may not make multiple Craft checks for the same
  crafting project within the same downtime activity.

- **Reallocation.** When you make a Craft check, you may expend a
  Resource to replace the d20 outcome with a 20.

features.name: Unified Model
features.flavor_text: You've unlocked the arcanum, exposing the smallest packet of magical energy. All the terms of the equation are now at your disposal.
features.description:
At 3rd Rank, after regaining expended Spell Points at the end of a long
rest, you may expend a number of Spell Points up to your Artificer Rank
to assign them as if they were IP. At the end of your next long rest,
all IP assigned by expending Spell Points are unassigned, you
immediately regain the Spell Points, and you may immediately assign them
again using **Unified Model**.

features.name: Magical Appraisal
features.flavor_text: The eye of the crafter is rarely wrong.
features.description:
At 3rd Rank, you gain the following benefits:

- **Sight.** You may cast **Keepsake's Calling**, and you may do so
  without needing to hold a spellcasting focus. As part of casting
  **Keepsake's Calling**, you may take the Sense Magic action as a free
  action before choosing a target object.

- **Source.** When you become aware of persistent magic through the
  Sense Magic action, if the persistent magic is on a spell list, you
  learn what the spell is regardless of which spell list it is on.

features.name: Ability Improvement
features.description:
At 3rd, 6th, and 9th Rank, you gain an Ability Improvement. Each time
you gain an Ability Improvement, distribute three permanent +1 increases
to your ability modifiers. You may increase the same ability modifier
multiple times.

The maximum ability modifier that may be achieved is limited by your
character's Level. The maximum is +5 at Level 3, increases to +6 at
Level 6, and finally becomes +7 at Level 9.

features.name: Exploration
features.description:
At 4th, 7th, and 10th Rank, you gain an Exploration with Artificer being
the exploring Class. Each time you gain an Exploration, you gain the
following benefit:

- **Bonus Rank.** You choose any Class other than Artificer and gain one
  Rank in the chosen Class.

features.name: Artificer Field Feature
features.description: At 4th Rank, you gain a feature for your Artificer Field.

features.name: Wondrous Workshop
features.flavor_text: Bigger on the inside, it's known as the Commercial, Resource, and Innovation Businessfront.
features.description:
At 5th Rank, as part of taking the Craft downtime activity, you may open
a door to a magical demiplane that serves as your workshop. The door
closes and disappears when you are done with the downtime activity.
While inside, you gain the following benefits:

- **Flexible Foundry.** You may ignore the **Specialization**
  requirement when starting a crafting project. Craft checks you make
  towards a project started in this way add half your Tier Bonus instead
  of your full Tier Bonus.

- **Interplanar Peddler.** Instead of adding progress to a crafting
  project, you may instead make a Craft check against a DC of 20. On a
  success, you acquire a Resource.

- **Secure Storage.** Crafting projects are held securely inside the
  **Wondrous Workshop** while it is no longer summoned.

features.name: Improvise II
features.flavor_text: A violent fizzling of magical power is followed by an amorphous spark of a spell dancing between your fingertips, ready to become something new.
features.description: At 6th Rank, gadgets you create with **Improvise** may now be Uncommon.

features.name: Spell Widgets I
features.flavor_text: As a spell application developer, you need some good quality assurance testers.
features.description:
At 6th Rank, at the end of a long rest, you may create 3 consumable
items called *spell widgets*. For each spell widget, you choose a spell
that you are able to prepare for the Artificer class to become its
*stored spell,* and you must expend Spell Points equal to the stored
spell's baseline Circle. You do not need to prepare any of the stored
spells and you may choose the same spell more than once.

A creature holding a spell widget may cast the stored spell as a Use
Item action at the spell's baseline Circle using your spellcasting
modifier. Unused spell widgets lose their stored spell at the end of the
next long rest.

features.name: Ability Improvement
features.description:
At 6th Rank, you gain an Ability Improvement. Each time you gain an
Ability Improvement, distribute three permanent +1 increases to your
ability modifiers. You may increase the same ability modifier multiple
times.

The maximum ability modifier that may be achieved is limited by your
character's Level. The maximum is +5 at Level 3, increases to +6 at
Level 6, and finally becomes +7 at Level 9.

features.name: Share Results I
features.flavor_text: You really feel like your exact perspective could be helpful right now.
features.description:
At 7th Rank, as a reaction when an ally within 15 feet of you makes an
INT or WIS combat check, you may add your INT to the total.

features.name: Exploration
features.description:
At 7th Rank, you gain an Exploration with Artificer being the exploring
Class. Each time you gain an Exploration, you gain the following
benefit:

- **Bonus Rank.** You choose any Class other than Artificer and gain one
  Rank in the chosen Class.

features.name: Fine-Tuned
features.flavor_text: Do you mind if I...yes, I know this wand makes flowers grow. What really is the difference between that and a fireball anyway?
features.description:
At 8th Rank, you are able to take the *Fine-Tune* downtime activity to
make a permanent modification to some of your party's Curios in one of
the following ways:

- **Equivalent.** You may choose a reforge trait on a Curio in your
  party's possession that has multiple variants, such as the
  **Energized** trait, and change the variant of the trait to one of
  your choice.

- **Exchange.** You swap the aspects of two Curios in your party's
  possession.

features.name: Artificer Field Feature
features.description: At 8th Rank, you gain a feature for your Artificer Field.

features.name: Spell Widgets II
features.flavor_text: Efficient power throughput for essential daily use!
features.description:
At 9th Rank, you no longer expend a Spell Point when storing a 1st
Circle spell on a spell widget.

features.name: Ability Improvement
features.description:
At 9th Rank, you gain an Ability Improvement. Each time you gain an
Ability Improvement, distribute three permanent +1 increases to your
ability modifiers. You may increase the same ability modifier multiple
times.

The maximum ability modifier that may be achieved is limited by your
character's Level. The maximum is +5 at Level 3, increases to +6 at
Level 6, and finally becomes +7 at Level 9.

features.name: Magical Reserves
features.flavor_text: Picking which item to sacrifice is difficult, as each creation is unique and beautiful in its own way.
features.description:
At 10th Rank, once per long rest as a free action, you may choose to
unassign a number of IP to produce one of the following effects:

- **Chain Reaction.** You unassign 5 IP and designate an item that was
  either created or modified by a feature that assigned IP. You deal
  your overload die as your choice of **elemental damage** to all
  subjects in a 10 foot radius around you or the item. You may activate
  this from any distance as long as you can **perceive** the item. If
  the item is a consumable, it is destroyed.

- **My Work for My Life.** When you are about to burn a Resolve, you may
  instead unassign 10 IP to gain the effects of burning a Resolve.

features.name: Exploration
features.description:
At 10th Rank, you gain an Exploration with Artificer being the exploring
Class. Each time you gain an Exploration, you gain the following
benefit:

- **Bonus Rank.** You choose any Class other than Artificer and gain one
  Rank in the chosen Class.

features.name: Improvise III
features.flavor_text: Did you know the potions you drink every day contain the same chemicals used in bombs?!
features.description: At 11th Rank, gadgets you create with **Improvise** may now be Rare.

features.name: Share Results II
features.flavor_text: Your advice was so clever last time, and clearly they need more of it.
features.description:
At 11th Rank, you may target allies up to 30 feet away with **Share
Results**.

features.name: Maximized Arcanodynamic Potential
features.flavor_text: The potency of spells has been bounded by the quantity of magic fueling it. It is therefore important to research self-generative magitechnology capable of producing greater outputs than inputs into the system.
features.description:
At 12th Rank, when you take the Craft downtime activity, you may spend
any number of Resources and complete an equal number of crafting
projects.

Additionally, your spell widgets no longer cost Spell Points to store
the spell.

features.name: Final Reach
features.description:
When you reach character Level 12, you compute your *Total Rank*, which
is calculated as:

###### The sum of all of your Class Ranks

If your Total Rank is less than 16 when you reach Level 12, then for
each deficient Rank, you may choose one of the benefits below to modify
a feature granted by one of your Class Ranks:

- **Maneuvers.** You learn two additional maneuvers.

- **Battlecasting.** You gain a maneuver, an additional Spell Point, and
  an additional prepared spell for that Class.

- **Spellcasting.** You gain three additional Spell Points and three
  additional prepared spells for that Class.

Artificer Fields

features.name: Artificer Field: Chemistry
features.flavor_text: Civilization's chief science concerns the classification, distillation, and production of the vital forces instrumental in mortal kind's survival. Whether it be the capture of fire to stave off the night, the purification of water to slake thirst, or the digging of the foundation for society, the elements persist and can be tamed.
features.description:
## Potioncraft

*Water, blood, and bile. Our body brews its potions, now we improve the
mechanism.*

At 1st Rank, you specialize in potion and remedy consumables and gain
the following benefits:

- You learn a stock recipe for a Common potion or remedy Curio.

- When creating items via stock recipes, you may choose an extra stock
  recipe for a potion or remedy and create its corresponding items.

- You may add the Crafting (Alchemist) specialization to your
  Adventuring Trade (this does not count towards the number of
  specializations on that trade).

- Your Tier Bonus is doubled for Craft checks made to craft potion and
  remedy Curios.

## Reagent Reserves

*Nature always intends and strives towards the perfection of gold, but
many accidents come between the change of metals.*

At 1st Rank, you have a certain number of *reagents* in your *reserves*
that you use to create potions and remedies. At the end of each long
rest, all unused reagents are lost and your reserves are restocked by
rolling your *reagent pool,* which is a number of d4s equal to twice
your Artificer Rank. Each natural value in the dice pool's outcome
grants an associated reagent as shown in the table below:

+------------+-------------------------------------+-------------+
| **Die**    | **Symbol**                          | **Reagent** |
|            |                                     |             |
| **Result** |                                     | **Name**    |
+------------+-------------------------------------+-------------+
| 1          | ![](media/image2.png){width="2.0in" | Fire        |
|            | height="2.0in"}                     |             |
+------------+-------------------------------------+-------------+
| 2          | ![](media/image1.png){width="2.0in" | Air         |
|            | height="2.0in"}                     |             |
+------------+-------------------------------------+-------------+
| 3          | ![](media/image4.png){width="2.0in" | Water       |
|            | height="2.0in"}                     |             |
+------------+-------------------------------------+-------------+
| 4          | ![](media/image3.png){width="2.0in" | Earth       |
|            | height="2.0in"}                     |             |
+============+=====================================+=============+

## Synthesis

After rolling your reagent pool, you may assign one or more IP to
specific reagents to add an equal number of reagents in any combination
to your reserves. You may only unassign IP assigned to unused reagents.

## Catalyzed Stock Recipe

*Each reaction has its own unique chemical pathway, twisting about
guarded secrets and around energetic hurdles. Your stockpile of potions
is undoubtedly your own.*

At 1st Rank, you may assign your IP to a *catalyzed stock recipe*, which
is added to your list of known stock recipes until you unassign the IP,
which also renders any items created by the stock recipe inert. You may
assign IP to catalyzed stock recipes multiple times, choosing a unique
Curio each time from the Curio Compendium. You must assign 1/2/3/4 IP
for a Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary item, and you are able to do so at
1st/4th/7th/10th Rank, respectively.

Each time you use a catalyzed stock recipe, you may either create its
chosen potion or remedy as its *catalyzed brew* or a general *craft
brew* (details further below).

### Catalyzed Brew

Each time you create a catalyzed brew, you must additionally consume
certain reagents from your reserves, following these Laws of Alchemy:

- **Law of Constant Composition.** You must consume at least 1 reagent
  for each IP assigned to the recipe.

- **Law of Equivalent Exchange.** If not determined by the following
  Laws, any reagent may be used to satisfy the **Law of Constant
  Composition**.

- **Law of Calcination.** You must consume a **fire**
  (![](media/image2.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"}) reagent if the
  Curio improves offensive combat abilities (e.g. attacks, dealing
  damage, critical hits, applying conditions).

- **Law of Dissolution.** You must consume a **water**
  (![](media/image4.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"}) reagent if the
  Curio causes the user to regain ST or heal HP.

- **Law of Separation.** You must consume an **air**
  (![](media/image1.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"}) reagent if the
  Curio cleanses conditions, increases Speed, or grants mobilities.

- **Law of Conjunction.** You must consume an **earth**
  (![](media/image3.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"}) reagent if the
  Curio improves defensive combat abilities (e.g. damage buffers, saving
  throw benefits, resisting conditions).

### Craft Brew

Each time you create a craft brew, you may simply combine reagents,
following the **Law of Constant Composition**, to produce two potions
that each grant the following benefits:

- **Fire.** Once per round until the end of their next short rest, when
  the drinker hits with an attack, they may add your overload die to the
  damage roll. Each extra ![](media/image2.png){width="0.2in"
  height="0.2in"} reagent consumed after the first adds an additional +5
  bonus to this damage roll.

- **Air.** Until the end of their next short rest, the drinker gains +10
  feet Speed for each ![](media/image1.png){width="0.2in"
  height="0.2in"} reagent consumed.

- **Water.** The drinker regains 10 ST and 5 HP for each
  ![](media/image4.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"} reagent consumed.

- **Earth.** Until the end of their next short rest, the drinker gains a
  DB 5 for each ![](media/image3.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"}
  reagent consumed against a single damage type.

If more than one of the same reagent is used for a craft brew, the
effects stack. Potion effects created by your craft brews that last
until the end of a next short rest, regardless of composition, all have
the same Toxicity effect:

- **Experimental.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you are
  poisoned.

## Tria Prima

*All of the seven metals are born from a threefold matter. Mercury is
the spirit, sulphur is the soul, and salt is the body. The soul unites
those two contraries, the body and spirit, and changes them into one
essence.*

At 4th Rank, your potions gain extra benefits when you combine reagents
in specific ways. If a pair of elements in the set you consumed to make
a **Catalyzed Stock Recipe** is listed below, you may choose to append
its saving throw bonuses to the benefits of the potion or remedy you
create.

- **Sulphur.** If ![](media/image2.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"}
  and ![](media/image1.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"} reagents are
  both present, the drinker additionally gains advantage on all **Will**
  and **Courage** saving throws until the end of the next short or long
  rest.

- **Mercury.** If ![](media/image1.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"}
  and ![](media/image4.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"} reagents are
  both present, the drinker additionally gains advantage on all
  **Reflex** and **Quick-Wits** saving throws until the end of the next
  short or long rest.

- **Salt.** If ![](media/image4.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"} and
  ![](media/image3.png){width="0.2in" height="0.2in"} reagents are both
  present, the drinker additionally gains advantage on all **Poise** and
  **Fortitude** saving throws until the end of the next short or long
  rest.

## Transmutation

*The goal of the ancients was to change lead to gold. I'll settle for a
healing potion.*

At 4th Rank, you may transmute the reagent dice pool before rolling it.
Assign 1 IP to this feature and choose a reagent. Any natural values
rolled that would give you the chosen reagent are instead replaced with
other reagents of your choice.

## Advanced Lab Techniques

*True mastery does not arise from the novelty of formulae but in the
efficiency and efficacy of the process.*

At 8th Rank, you gain the following improvements.

- **Fermentation.** You may replace the toxicity effect on potions
  created before your last long rest with the following:

- **Alcoholic.** You gain disadvantage on **Perceive** checks.

- **Distillation.** Immediately before creating items with stock
  recipes, you may unassign IP from a Catalyzed Stock Recipe of your
  choice, rendering any items created by it since your last long rest
  inert. Doing so refunds the reagents used to make them.

- **Coagulation.** You may take three actions to create a single item
  from one of your Catalyzed Stock Recipes using your reagents.

features.name: Artificer Field: Engraving
features.flavor_text: Your craftwork is the art of metal, of smoke, of calligraphy, and most of all, of persistence. Hours have been poured into the exact heft of the handle, the honed sharpness of its edge, and the intricacies of the fizzling runes lining the prototype. Words have power, lending their weight to the blow.
features.description:
## Runecraft

*Blood, sweat, and tears temper the steel of your work. Your dreams are
alloyed with your plans, and they begin to take shape.*

At 1st Rank, you specialize in weapon equipment and gain the following
benefits:

- You acquire a Common weapon Curio purposed for melee fighting styles.

- You may add the Crafting (Battlesmith) specialization to your
  Adventuring Trade (this does not count towards the number of
  specializations on that trade).

- Your Tier Bonus is doubled for Craft checks made to craft weapons
  purposed for melee fighting styles and upgrades for them.

## Energized Runes

*The last careful etchings into the steel provide the final touches
required to ignite the blade with power.*

At 1st Rank, at the end of each of your long rests, you may assign your
IP to weapons purposed for melee fighting styles to append additional
reforge traits called *engraver traits* to the *engraved weapon* (the
List of Engraver Traits is described further below). You may assign 1 or
3 IP to each engraver trait.

When you make a melee weapon attack with an engraved weapon, you may
*invoke* one of the engraver traits and convert all damage dealt by the
attack to the damage type of the trait, adding any additional effects as
listed in its description.

Once per round when you hit with an attack for which you invoked an
engraver trait, you may also add your overload die to the damage dealt
by the attack.

## Intensified Inlays

*As pronunciation matters to chanted magic, so the diacritics hone the
inscribed.*

At 4th Rank, you may now assign 1, 3, 5, or 7 IP to an engraver trait.

## Runic Overdrive

*In a fantastic upgrade, you line the weapon with power channels able to
redirect and concentrate its energies at the convenience of an array of
tenuously connected knobs and dials.*

At 8th Rank, as a free action, you may unassign the IP of one of your
engraver traits to regain the usage of the aspect of the engraved
weapon. If you do so immediately before using the aspect, you may add
the engraver trait's listed *aspect booster* to the aspect's effect.

## List of Engraver Traits

### Caustic Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*Deep grooves are impressed by multitudes of delicate brushstrokes, all
painting the hilt with corrosive acid.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **acid damage**. If you have assigned 3/5/7 IP
  to this engraver trait, the attack's critical hit threshold is also
  reduced by 1/2/3.

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** All affected creatures have their AC
  reduced by one guard step (they either have their Full Guard AC, Half
  Guard AC, or Base AC). This effect has the **Recoverable** condition
  trait, allowing an action to end the effect.

### Frigid Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*Your breath can be seen in the air around the cold steel made colder by
frozen script.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **cold damage**. If you have assigned 3/5/7 IP
  to this engraver trait and the attack hits, the target's Speed is
  reduced by 5/10/15 feet until the end of its next turn.

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** All affected creatures become
  stuck with the **Recoverable** condition trait, allowing
  an action to end the effect.

### Pyral Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*Almost too hot to handle.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **fire damage**. If you have assigned 3/5/7 IP
  to this engraver trait and the attack hits, it deals an additional
  **1d6/2d6/3d6 damage**.

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** All affected creatures are set
  ablaze.

### Ethereal Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*Wisps of surreal smoke waft out of the planar stabilization vents.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **force damage**. If you have assigned 3/5/7 IP
  to this engraver trait and the attack hits, you may add a +2/4/6 bonus
  to the next saving throw you make against a spell effect before the
  start of your next turn.

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** All affected creatures fall
  prone.

### Electric Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*At first you thought it wouldn't be an issue to forgo insulated grips
on the prototype.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **lightning damage**. If you have assigned
  3/5/7 IP to this engraver trait and the attack hits, you may add
  10/20/30 feet to your movement pool.

<!-- -->

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** Immediately before the aspect's effect
  occurs, you may teleport to a target unoccupied space within 30 feet
  of you.

### Virulent Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*Every culture's tradition for what happens after life is included on
this list of dark patterns.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **necrotic damage**. If you have assigned 3/5/7
  IP to this engraver trait and the attack hits, you may **regain
  5/10/15 ST**.

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** You **regain ST** equal to half of all of
  the damage all creatures take from the aspect's effect.

### Venomous Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*The slow drip of the lurid fang drives a primal fear into those who
witness it.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **poison damage**. If you have assigned 3/5/7
  IP to this engraver trait and the attack hits, the target of the
  attack incurs a -1/-2/-3 penalty to the next attack check they make
  before the start of your next turn.

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** All affected creatures become
  poisoned until the end of their next turn.

### Illusory Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*Only visible under a new moon or when the weapon is feeling cheeky.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **psychic damage**. If you have assigned 3/5/7
  IP to this engraver trait and the attack hits, the target of the
  attack is dealt **1d10/2d10/3d10 psychic damage** each time it attacks
  you before the start of your next turn.

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** All affected creatures gain disadvantage
  on all attacks against you until the start of your next turn.

### Sunlit Rune

Artificer Engraver Trait

*Soft light plays off of the diamond chisel as it carves the symbols,
illuminated as bright as the sun itself.*

- **Invoke.** When you invoke this engraver trait, all of the attack's
  damage is converted to **radiant damage**. If you have assigned 3/5/7
  IP to this engraver trait and the attack hits, the attack deals
  **6/8/10 radiant damage** to an additional creature within 5 feet of
  the attack's target.

- **8th Rank Aspect Booster.** All affected creatures become
  disoriented until the end of their next turn.

features.name: Artificer Field: Wandcarving
features.flavor_text: Oak for its sturdiness. Maple for its reliability. Willow for its whimsy. Regardless of the choice, the grain of possibility runs along its length. The scraps that curl off of the board as it is planed down to size smell of the deep magic in the forest of its birth. Even the sawdust from its turning maintains a hint of enchantment.
features.description:
## Wandcraft

*The spellcaster's stick, the legerdemain's ledger; whatever its shape,
you can make it.*

At 1st Rank, you specialize in spellcasting focus equipment and gain the
following benefits:

- You acquire a Common spellcasting focus Curio.

- You may add the Crafting (Scrivener) specialization to your
  Adventuring Trade (this does not count towards the number of
  specializations on that trade).

- Your Tier Bonus is doubled for Craft checks made to craft spellcasting
  foci and upgrades for them.

## Chaser Bolt

*Mundane wands absorb the shock of sorcery, but when magic already
permeates every pore of the wood through which spells are channeled, the
addition of a safety release valve is necessary. My safety, not theirs.*

At 1st Rank, once per rest when you cast a spell, you may add your
overload die to the spell's damage roll.

## Expanded Imbuement

*The smallest packet of quantized magic easily finds its home in
existing arcanergetic infrastructure.*

At 1st Rank, at the end of each of your long rests, you may assign your
IP to one or more spellcasting focus's **Imbued Spells** aspect to
append additional Arcane spells to its spell list. Each spell you append
to the list requires assigning IP equal to its baseline Circle and adds
the same number of Spell Charges to the aspect.

The baseline Circle of an appended spell has the following requirements:

- **Bounded.** The appended spell's Circle cannot be higher than the
  spell with the highest baseline Circle in the aspect's spell list.

- **Preparable.** The appended spell's Circle must be of a Circle you
  are capable of preparing for the Artificer Class.

You may heighten spells you cast using the Imbued Spells aspect up to
the highest baseline Circle in the aspect's spell list.

## Weaponized Wands

*Wands slung low flick in and out of holsters with dizzying speed,
peppering the enemy with magical munitions.*

At 4th Rank, spellcasting foci now count for you as weapons purposed for
the Basic Ranged fighting style without the **Ammunition** trait.

Whenever you cast a cantrip that makes a ranged spell attack, you may
*weaponize* it, granting it the following benefits:

- **Attack Type.** The weaponized cantrip attack is converted from a
  ranged spell attack to a ranged weapon attack using the Basic Ranged
  fighting style.

- **Damage Roll.** The weaponized cantrip spell damage roll is replaced
  by a weapon attack damage roll, as calculated below:

Your Martial Die + your DEX + your Tier Bonus

- **Damage Type.** The type of damage dealt by the spellcasting focus is
  the same as that of the weaponized cantrip's spell damage roll.

- **No Discharge Trait.** If the cantrip has the discharge trait (as
  noted in its description), you may not use it.

Additionally, whenever you use a spellcasting focus to make a weapon
attack with the Basic Ranged fighting style, you may instead cast a
weaponized cantrip (using any feature you have) as above.

## Elaborate Lathework

*The mother of magical machines.*

At 8th Rank, your spellcasting focus Imbued Aspects are improved in the
following ways:

- **Reload.** When you score a critical hit with a weaponized cantrip
  attack, your Imbued Spells aspect regains 1 Spell Charge.

- **High Caliber Imbuement.** When you append spells to an Imbued Spells
  aspect using the **Expanded Imbuement** feature, you may ignore the
  **Preparable** requirement for a single spell.
